goal:
You are working on a real-world software engineering task from SWE-Skills-Bench.

The task description, target files, requirements, and acceptance criteria are stored at /workspace/TASK.md inside your Docker workspace. Read it first with read_file_real.

The source tree is available at: /workspace/upgradle

Inspect the repository with shell_exec_in_workspace and read_file_real. Make changes with apply_patch when possible, or write_file_real when replacing a whole file. Use absolute paths for file tools.

After you finish, respond with TASK_COMPLETE.

--- TASK BRIEF (excerpt) ---
# Task: Fix ESLint Violations in TypeScript Codebase

## Background

The upgradle project uses TypeScript + ESLint for code quality enforcement. Currently, the `src/` directory contains multiple ESLint rule violations that need to be addressed:

- `no-unused-vars`
- `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any`
- `eqeqeq` (strict equality)

## Objective

Scan and fix all lint errors in `.ts` files under the `src/` directory to ensure the codebase passes linting checks.

## Scope

- **Files to modify**: `src/**/*.ts` (all TypeScript files in src directory)
- **Files to preserve**: Do NOT modify any test files

- **Repo requirements**: Ensure a `package.json` exists with `lint` and `test` scripts and a `src/` directory containing one or more `.ts` files so the test harness can run.

## Requirements

- Fix all ESLint error-level violations
- Maintain existing functionality (all existing tests must continue to pass)
- Follow TypeScript best practices
- Replace `any` types with proper type definitions where possible
- Use strict equality (`===`) instead of loose equality (`==`)
- Remove or properly use unused variables

## Acceptance Criteria

- `npm run lint` exits with code 0 (no error-level reports)

- No new lint warnings introduced
--- END BRIEF ---

function.name: read_file_real
function.description: Read a file from the live Docker workspace. Read /workspace/TASK.md first.

function.name: write_file_real
function.description: Write or overwrite a file in the live Docker workspace. Use absolute paths rooted at /workspace.

function.name: shell_exec_in_workspace
function.description: Run a shell command in the live Docker workspace and return stdout plus stderr.

function.name: apply_patch
function.description: Apply a unified diff to the repository in the Docker workspace using patch -p1 from /workspace.

workspace.setup_command: if [ -d /workspace/upgradle/.git ]; then cd /workspace/upgradle && git checkout HEAD -- . && git clean -fd; fi
